MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
Off Calendar Jun-27-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
John Hillsman, Esq. (415-421-9292) of McGuinn, Hillsman, Palefsky for Plaintiff.
Katelyn Knight, Esq. (415-524-4300) of Murchison & Cumming for Defendant Wilmer Oils.
Jennifer Porter, Esq. (415-398-6000) of Keesal, Young & Logan for Defendant Milestone and ISS Marine.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court informs the parties and cites rules why San Francisco is the proper venue in this highly technical case; There is no California decision on point. Defendant argues CCP 395(A). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT WILMAR OILS & FATS, LLC'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE IS DENIED. Wilmar has not shown that venue in San Francisco is improper under any applicable theory. When a non-California corporation without a principal place of business in California is sued, the suit may be brought in any county in California. (CCP 395(a); Easton v. Superior Court (1970) 12 Cal.App.3d 243, 246.) Defendants Waterbird Navigation, S.A., Milestone Chemical Tankers America, Inc., and TM Shipmanagment Company Ltd. are foreign corporations without a principal place of business in California. Nor has Wilmar established that transferring this action to San Joaquin County would promote convenience of witness or the ends of justice. Wilmar has not provided sufficient evidence regarding the witnesses that is required for a motion to change venue on the grounds of convenience of the witnesses. (Weil & Brown, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (The Rutter Group 2016) 3:576-577). (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) Order is signed. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/HK)
